en.extract:
The rufous-fronted babbler (Cyanoderma rufifrons) is a babbler species in the Old World babbler family. It occurs in the Eastern Himalayan foothills, Myanmar, Thailand, northern Indochina and south to the Malay Peninsula and the islands of Sumatra and Borneo. The buff-chested babbler is now subsumed into this species.


Taxonomy
The rufous-fronted babbler was formally described in 1873 by the English naturalist Allan Octavian Hume based on a specimen that had been collected by Eugene W. Oates on the western slopes of the Pegu Range of central Myanmar. Hume placed the specimen in the genus Stachyris and coined the binomial name Stachyris rufifrons. The specific epithet rufifrons is modern Latin meaning "red-fronted", from Latin rufus meaning "red" or "rufous" and frons, frontis meaning "forehead" or "brow". The rufous-fronted babbler is now one of seven babblers placed in the genus Cyanoderma that was first introduced in 1874 by the Italian zoologist Tommaso Salvadori.
Nine subspecies are recognised:

C. r. ambiguum (Harington, 1915) – east Himalayas, northwest Myanmar and northeast India
C. r. planicola (Mayr, 1941) – northeast Myanmar and southwest China
C. r. adjunctum (Deignan, 1939) – north, east Thailand and north Indochina
C. r. insuspectum (Deignan, 1939) – south Laos
C. r. pallescens Ticehurst, 1932 – west Myanmar
C. r. rufifrons (Hume, 1873) – southeast Myanmar and west Thailand
C. r. obscurum Baker, ECS, 1917 – south Myanmar and southwest Thailand
C. r. poliogaster (Hume, 1880) – south Malay Peninsula, Sumatra and north, central Borneo
C. r. sarawacense Chasen, 1939 – northwest Borneo
The first four subspecies on the above list (ambiguum, planicola, adjunctum and insuspectum) were formerly sometimes treated as a separate species, the buff-chested babbler (Cyanoderma ambiguum).
The binomial name Stachyris rodolphei was proposed by Herbert Girton Deignan in 1939 for three babbler specimens collected at Doi Chiang Dao in Thailand. The name is now considered synonymous with the nominate subspecies C. r. rufifrons.


Description
It is buff-brown with paler brown underparts and a dull rufous crown. Its upper wings, tail, supercilium and lores are whitish-grey. It is 12 cm (4.7 in) long and weighs 9–12 g (0.32–0.42 oz). Its song is a high-pitched tuh tuh-tuh-tuh-tuh-tuh.


References

The '''rufous-fronted babbler''' ('''''Cyanoderma rufifrons''''') is a babbler species in the [[Old World babbler]] family. It occurs in the [[Eastern Himalaya]]n foothills, Myanmar, Thailand, northern [[Indochina]] and south to the [[Malay Peninsula]] and the islands of [[Sumatra]] and [[Borneo]]. The buff-chested babbler is now subsumed into this species.

==Taxonomy==
The rufous-fronted babbler was [[species description|formally described]] in 1873 by the English naturalist [[Allan Octavian Hume]] based on a specimen that had been collected by [[Eugene W. Oates]] on the western slopes of the [[Pegu Range]] of central [[Myanmar]]. Hume placed the specimen in the genus ''[[Stachyris]]'' and coined the [[binomial name]] ''Stachyris rufifrons''.<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Hume | first=Allan Octavian | author-link=Allan Octavian Hume | date=1873 | title=Novelties – ''Stachyris rufifrons'', Nov. Sp. | journal=Stray Feathers | volume=1 | issue=6 | pages=479–480 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/30007391 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor1-last=Mayr | editor1-first=Ernst | editor1-link=Ernst Mayr | editor2-last=Paynter | editor2-first=Raymond A. Jr | year=1964 | title=Check-List of Birds of the World | volume=10 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology  | place=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=303 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14486492 }}</ref> The specific epithet ''rufifrons'' is [[modern Latin]] meaning "red-fronted", from [[Latin]] ''rufus'' meaning "red" or "rufous" and ''frons'', ''frontis'' meaning "forehead" or "brow".<ref>{{ cite web | last=Jobling | first=James A. | title=rufifrons | work=The Key to Scientific Names | url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/key-to-scientific-names/search?q=rufifrons | publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology | access-date=9 September 2024 }}</ref> The rufous-fronted babbler is now one of seven babblers placed in the genus ''[[Cyanoderma]]'' that was first introduced in 1874 by the Italian zoologist [[Tommaso Salvadori]].<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela C. Rasmussen | date=August 2024 | title=Babblers & fulvettas | work=IOC World Bird List Version 14.2 | url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/babblers/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=9 November 2024 }}</ref>
  
Nine [[subspecies]] are recognised:<ref name=ioc/>
* ''C. r. ambiguum'' ([[Herbert Hastings Harington|Harington]], 1915) – east Himalayas, northwest Myanmar and northeast India<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Harington | first=H.H. | author-link=Herbert Hastings Harington | date=1914 | title=Notes on the Indian Timeliides and their allies (laughing thrushes, babblers, &c.) Part IV. Family Timeliidæ | journal=Journal of the Bombay Natural History Society | volume=23 | pages=614–657 [628, 631] | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/30454098 }}</ref>
* ''C. r. planicola'' ([[Ernst Mayr|Mayr]], 1941) – northeast Myanmar and southwest China
* ''C. r. adjunctum'' ([[Herbert Girton Deignan|Deignan]], 1939) – north, east Thailand and north Indochina
* ''C. r. insuspectum'' (Deignan, 1939) – south Laos
* ''C. r. pallescens'' [[Claud B. Ticehurst|Ticehurst]], 1932 – west Myanmar
* ''C. r. rufifrons'' ([[Allan Octavian Hume|Hume]], 1873) – southeast Myanmar and west Thailand
* ''C. r. obscurum'' [[E. C. Stuart Baker|Baker, ECS]], 1917 – south Myanmar and southwest Thailand
* ''C. r. poliogaster'' (Hume, 1880) – south [[Malay Peninsula]], [[Sumatra]] and north, central [[Borneo]]
* ''C. r. sarawacense'' [[Frederick Nutter Chasen|Chasen]], 1939 – northwest [[Borneo]]

The first four subspecies on the above list (''ambiguum'', ''planicola'', ''adjunctum'' and ''insuspectum'') were formerly sometimes treated as a separate species, the buff-chested babbler (''Cyanoderma ambiguum'').<ref name=ioc/><ref>{{ cite web | last1=Clements | first1=J.F.| author1-link=James Clements (ornithologist) | last2=Rasmussen | first2=P.C. | author2-link=Pamela C. Rasmussen | last3=Schulenberg | first3=T.S. | last4=Iliff | first4=M.J. | last5=Fredericks | first5=T.A. | last6=Gerbracht | first6=J.A. | last7=Lepage | first7=D. | last8=Spencer | first8=A. | last9=Billerman | first9=S.M. | last10=Sullivan | first10=B.L. | last11=Smith | first11=M. | last12=Wood | first12=C.L. | year=2024 | title=The eBird/Clements checklist of birds of the world: v2024 | url=https://www.birds.cornell.edu/clementschecklist/download/ | access-date=26 October 2024 }}</ref>

The binomial name ''Stachyris rodolphei'' was proposed by [[Herbert Girton Deignan]] in 1939 for three babbler [[zoological specimen|specimen]]s collected at [[Doi Chiang Dao]] in Thailand. The name is now considered [[Synonym (taxonomy)|synonym]]ous with the [[nominate subspecies]] ''C. r. rufifrons''.<ref>{{cite journal |last1=Collar |first1=N. J. |year=2006 |title=A partial revision of the Asian babblers (Timaliidae) |journal=Forktail |issue=22 |pages=85–112 [106–107] |url=https://orientalbirdclub.org/wp-content/uploads/2012/09/Collar-Babbler.pdf}}</ref>

==Description==
It is buff-brown with paler brown underparts and a dull rufous crown. Its upper wings, tail, [[supercilium]] and [[Lore (anatomy)|lore]]s are whitish-grey. It is {{convert|12|cm|abbr=on}} long and weighs {{convert|9-12|g|abbr=on}}. Its song is a high-pitched ''tuh tuh-tuh-tuh-tuh-tuh''.<ref name=bow>{{cite journal | last1=Collar | first1=N. | last2=Robson | first2=C. | last3=Kirwan | first3=G.M. | last4=Boesman | first4=P.F.D. | date=2024 | title=Rufous-fronted Babbler (Cyanoderma rufifrons), version 2.1 | editor1-last=Keeney | editor1-first=B.K. | editor2-last=Billerman | editor2-first=S.M. | journal=Birds of the World | location=Ithaca, NY, USA | publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology | doi=10.2173/bow.rufbab10.02.1 }}</ref>

zh.extract:
红额穗鹛（学名：Stachyridopsis rufifrons），是画眉科的一种，分布于缅甸、马来西亚、泰国和印度尼西亚。该物种的保护状况被评为无危。
红额穗鹛的平均体重约为9.7克。栖息地包括亚热带或热带的（低地）湿润疏灌丛、亚热带或热带的（低地）季节性湿润草原、亚热带或热带的湿润低地林、种植园、亚热带或热带严重退化的前森林和温带森林。


参考文献
